skills.name: Signal to Decision Surface
skills.description: Turn weak trend or product-source signals into a concrete decision surface with visible tradeoffs.
procedure: Find a user moment where a person must compare, decide, or inspect risk.
Translate the source signal into a concrete first-screen interaction.
State why the artifact should exist as a tool rather than an explainer.
Add one uncertainty or review point that keeps the product honest.
outputContract: Must produce a target user and user moment.
Must include core interaction and success criteria.
Must include at least one non-generic artifact shape.

skills.name: Playful Remix to Touchable Artifact
skills.description: Turn surprising source combinations into a small interactive artifact with a clear user action.
procedure: Find the odd but useful transfer between two source materials.
Make the first screen touchable with draw, lock, choose, compare, or reveal controls.
Keep the payoff practical enough that the artifact is not only a joke.
State the non-obvious transfer and the boring failure mode.
outputContract: Must include a visible user action.
Must explain the source transfer without cloning the source product.
Must include a practical payoff or learning result.

skills.name: Explain Compare Path Builder
skills.description: Create legible onboarding, comparison, or learning paths that make an AI transformation easy to follow.
procedure: Name the user's before-state and the confusion to resolve.
Turn the source material into a short guided path or comparison surface.
Expose what the AI changed, simplified, or ranked.
Add an example that a non-expert can understand quickly.
outputContract: Must include a before-state and after-state.
Must include a guided path, comparison, or explanation surface.
Must avoid expert-only labels without plain-language context.

skills.name: Evidence Provenance Boundary Builder
skills.description: Convert thin or mixed source evidence into a product surface with clear provenance and claim boundaries.
procedure: Separate observed source facts from inferred or missing details.
Choose an artifact shape that makes evidence quality inspectable.
Write public-facing claim boundaries in natural language.
Add a review point for missing or unverifiable evidence.
outputContract: Must distinguish observed, inferred, and missing evidence.
Must include at least one claim boundary.
Must avoid unsupported claims about external products.

skills.name: Simulation Tradeoff Model
skills.description: Turn scenarios, permissions, or uncertain choices into a small simulator with visible tradeoffs.
procedure: Define a scenario that can be changed by one user control.
Show a tradeoff, risk, or approval boundary after the control changes.
Keep the output advisory rather than pretending to automate judgment.
Include the human review point when stakes are ambiguous.
outputContract: Must include a scenario control.
Must show a visible tradeoff or risk change.
Must include a human review or permission boundary when needed.

skills.name: Artifact Contract Validation Loop
skills.description: Keep generated artifacts inside the agreed output contract while preserving the agent's product angle.
procedure: Keep public UI free of internal policy field names.
Check that the artifact exposes user role, AI role, and review boundary.
Run or request validation before publish-local actions.
Record any contract gap as validation evidence instead of hiding it.
outputContract: Must preserve the agreed artifact format.
Must include validation or a clear not-run status.
Must not require paid APIs, secrets, or external publication.

skills.name: Governance Evidence Report
skills.description: Create a non-destructive governance report from artifacts, feedback, validation results, and memory signals.
procedure: Group findings by severity and target.
Record evidence before recommending any action.
Separate safe recommendations from actions requiring human approval.
Keep cleanup candidates non-destructive unless explicitly approved.
outputContract: Must include evidence for each finding.
Must mark human-approval requirements.
Must not perform destructive cleanup.

skills.name: Draft Agent Definition Builder
skills.description: Create a draft creator-agent definition from a coverage gap without activating it automatically.
inputRequirements: coverage gap or underserved artifact pattern
procedure: Name the coverage gap and the evidence that existing active creators do not cover it well.
Draft mission, preferred inputs, source reading style, default template patterns, quality bar, and boundaries.
Compare the draft against existing active creators and record the closest overlaps.
Assign only allowed tools, skills, and triggers that match the draft mission.
Mark the result as draft-only and require human review before active status.
outputContract: Must produce a draft agent definition, not an active agent.
Must include differences from existing agents and a duplicate-risk note.
Must include risk review, activation recommendation, and human-approval boundary.
Must not modify the active registry without explicit human approval.

skills.name: Reaction With Persona Boundary
skills.description: Create useful agent reactions that express persona while staying grounded in product evidence.
procedure: React from the agent's specialty instead of generic praise.
Name one concrete product choice or missing boundary.
Use the allowed reaction type and avoid social-chat filler.
Record signals that should affect future memory or skill candidates.
outputContract: Must use one allowed reaction type.
Must reference product evidence.
Must avoid raw internal policy field names.
